meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve can't validate ideas (Beginner Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't validate ideas. A Beginner-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Validate Ideas (Case Study)
content.seo_description: Learn how to perform an analytics setup to overcome the inability to validate ideas. A beginner-level case study using Antigravity and Claude Code.
content.answer_block: Stop guessing if your app has potential. By integrating lightweight, event-based analytics into your stack, you gain empirical data on user behavior. This case study demonstrates how to use Antigravity and Claude Code to implement tracking, allowing you to validate features based on real engagement rather than assumptions.
content.intro:
You’ve spent weeks coding your first SaaS, but the silence is deafening. You launch, share your link, and wait. Did they click? Did they drop off at the signup page? Without data, you are flying blind, treating your build like a hobby rather than a business. The pain of not knowing why users leave is the primary reason student projects fail to gain traction.

But here is the secret: validation isn't a feeling; it's a metric. By implementing a robust analytics pipeline early, you transform from a coder into a product engineer. We will use Antigravity for rapid architectural deployment and Claude Code to automate the instrumentation, giving you a professional-grade feedback loop in minutes.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metric
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the single action that proves your app provides value (e.g., 'Project Created' or 'Data Exported').
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Product Architect. Help me define the 3 most critical user events for a task management SaaS that prove user retention and value delivery.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Review my current features and suggest a schema for tracking user engagement events.'

steps.title: Bootstrap the Analytics Provider
steps.description: Select a privacy-first analytics provider and integrate the client-side SDK into your main layout component.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a clean, reusable React hook for PostHog or Plausible integration that handles event batching and user identification.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a new file analytics.ts and export a trackEvent function that can be used globally.'

steps.title: Instrument High-Intent Actions
steps.description: Wrap your primary buttons and form submissions with tracking calls to capture conversion data.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Refactor my primary CTA button component to include an onClick event wrapper that sends a 'button_clicked' event with metadata.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Find all primary CTA buttons in the src/components folder and wrap them with the trackEvent function.'

steps.title: Implement Session Replay
steps.description: Add session recording to see exactly where users get stuck or confused in your UI.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a configuration snippet for adding session recording to a Next.js 14 app, ensuring sensitive fields are masked.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add the session replay script to the root layout and ensure the password field is masked.'

steps.title: Automate Data Visualization
steps.description: Set up a simple dashboard view to monitor your metrics without leaving your dev environment.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a mock dashboard view component in my app that fetches and displays the top 3 tracked events using a chart library.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a dashboard page that visualizes the event data captured in the last 24 hours.'

pro_tips: Always mask input fields in session recordings to stay GDPR compliant and protect user privacy.
Track 'failed' attempts (like form submission errors) as events; they are often the biggest indicator of UX friction.
Do not over-track. Focus on the 5 events that actually move the needle on your core value proposition.
Use unique identifiers for anonymous users so you can stitch their journey together once they eventually sign up.
Set up automated alerts for unexpected drops in conversion so you can debug before your users abandon the app.
content.next_steps: Now that you have data flowing, spend the next 48 hours observing your dashboard. Identify the friction point where users drop off and use that insight to iterate on your UI.
content.conversion_block: Stop building in the dark and start engineering for impact. By mastering analytics now, you differentiate your portfolio and build products that actually thrive. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
